name: generate_chart
description: Generate an interactive Plotly chart from row-dict data. PREFER THIS over python_execute for standard charts — it is more reliable in the sandbox. 12 chart types: bar, bar_horizontal, line, area, scatter, histogram, pie, donut, heatmap, box, treemap, waterfall. Pass rows directly from duckdb_query, iris tools, or any tool result. Key params: data (list of row dicts), chart_type, x (column name), y (column name or list for multi-series), color (column for series split), theme (riskgpt|minimal|dark), bar_mode (group|stack), title, x_label, y_label. Returns self-contained HTML rendered in canvas panel.

data.description: List of row dicts, e.g. [{"month":"Jan","revenue":5000},{...}]. Also accepts a data_transform result object directly (tool extracts .data key automatically).

x.description: Column name to use for the x-axis or category labels.

y.description: Column name (string) or list of column names (array) for the y-axis / values. Use an array for multi-series line/bar charts.

title.description: Chart title displayed at the top.

color.description: Column name to split data into colour-coded series (one trace per unique value).

bar_mode.description: Bar grouping mode. Only applies to bar/bar_horizontal charts.

line_shape.description: Line interpolation. Only applies to line/area charts.

save_html.description: Save chart as standalone .html file to my_data/ and return html_file path.

save_png.description: Export PNG at 2x resolution via Kaleido. Returns png_path.

filename.description: Output filename (no extension). Defaults to chart_{type}_{timestamp}.

html.description: Self-contained HTML string with embedded Plotly.js. Render directly in canvas.

png_path.description: Absolute path to saved PNG (only if save_png=true).

html_file.description: Absolute path to saved HTML (only if save_html=true).
